skill.name: flutter-expert
skill.description: Master Flutter development with Dart 3, advanced widgets, and multi-platform deployment. Handles state management, animations, testing, and performance optimization for mobile, web, desktop, and embedded platforms.
skill.summary: Expert Flutter developer for multi-platform app development with Dart 3 and Flutter 3.x+
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 potential security issues. Risk score: 0/100. This is a benign Flutter development assistant skill that provides guidance, best practices, and code examples through natural language prompts only.

content.value_statement: Flutter developers need expert guidance on architecture, state management, and performance optimization across multiple platforms. This skill provides comprehensive Flutter 3.x and Dart 3 expertise for building production-ready applications.
actual_capabilities: Provide expert guidance on Flutter 3.x and Dart 3 best practices
Recommend and implement state management solutions (Riverpod, Bloc, GetX)
Design clean architecture patterns for Flutter applications
Optimize performance for mobile, web, desktop, and embedded platforms
Create advanced UI/UX with custom painters, animations, and responsive design
Implement platform channels for native iOS/Android integration
limitations: Does not execute or run Flutter/Dart code
Cannot build, compile, or deploy applications
Cannot provide real-time device debugging or profiling
Cannot access external APIs or services on behalf of users

use_cases.title: Architecture Design
use_cases.description: Get guidance on structuring Flutter apps with clean architecture, dependency injection, and modular code organization

use_cases.title: State Management Selection
use_cases.description: Receive recommendations for state management solutions based on app complexity and team experience
use_cases.target_user: Teams evaluating Flutter state management options

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Learn techniques for optimizing widget rebuilds, memory usage, and rendering performance

prompt_templates.title: Basic Flutter Question
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I implement [specific widget or feature] in Flutter?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner developers needing quick widget implementation guidance

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a clean architecture structure for a Flutter app that needs [feature list]. What folders, patterns, and dependencies should I use?
prompt_templates.scenario: Developers planning new Flutter project structure

prompt_templates.title: State Management Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement state management for a [describe app feature] using [Riverpod/Bloc/GetX]. Include code examples for state, events, and UI consumption.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate developers building complex state-driven features

prompt_templates.title: Platform Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a platform channel to access [native iOS/Android feature] from Flutter. Include both Dart and native code implementation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced developers needing native platform features

output_examples.input: How do I implement a responsive layout that works on mobile and desktop?
What's the best state management for a large e-commerce app?
output_examples.output: For large e-commerce apps, I recommend Riverpod 2.x or Bloc. Riverpod offers compile-time safety and excellent testing support. Bloc provides clear separation of events and states. Consider a hybrid: Riverpod for dependency injection and global state, Bloc for complex business logic in feature modules.

best_practices: Use const constructors wherever possible to optimize widget rebuilds
Implement proper error handling with try-catch and custom Exception classes
Write comprehensive tests: unit tests for business logic, widget tests for UI components
anti_patterns: Avoid using setState() for complex state - use proper state management instead
Do not ignore the 'const' keyword on widgets that accept constant children
Avoid rebuilding entire widget trees - use Selector or Consumer for granular updates

faq.question: Does this skill work with Flutter Web?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill covers Flutter web deployment, PWA configuration, and web-specific optimizations.

faq.question: Can this help with iOS and Android specific code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes platform channel creation, Swift/Kotlin integration, and native plugin development.

faq.question: What Dart version does this support?
faq.answer: This skill covers Dart 3.x features including records, patterns, sealed classes, and null safety.

faq.question: Can this skill write complete app code?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance, code examples, and architecture recommendations. It does not execute or build code.

faq.question: Does it cover testing strategies?
faq.answer: Yes. Includes unit testing, widget testing, integration testing, and golden file testing best practices.

faq.question: Can this help with performance optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. Covers Impeller optimization, widget rebuild minimization, memory profiling, and build size reduction.
